evals.prompt: We have a Go background service processing 2,000 Kafka events per minute. Each event triggers a single PostgreSQL row lookup by ID. p95 latency is spiking to 800ms under load. Connection pool is set to 20. Should I add Redis caching or switch to batch reads?
evals.expected_output: Pattern Guidance response with mode label, BotE pool math (query_count x hold_time vs pool size), when to use / when NOT to use for both options, stack-specific (.NET/Go/PostgreSQL) trade-offs, and a concrete decision rule.

assertions.text: Response includes 'Mode: Pattern Guidance' or 'Pattern Guidance' as a heading
assertions.name: contains-mode-label

assertions.text: Response shows BotE pool math with formula involving query_count x hold_time vs pool size, with actual numbers
assertions.name: contains-bote-pool-math

assertions.text: Response addresses both Redis caching AND batch reads as options
assertions.name: covers-both-options

assertions.text: Response includes a 'when NOT to use' section for at least one option
assertions.name: has-when-not-to-use

assertions.text: Response has a concrete if/then decision rule (not just 'it depends')
assertions.name: has-decision-rule

evals.prompt: Our .NET API started throwing 'Timeout expired. The timeout period elapsed prior to obtaining a connection from the pool.' yesterday at 3pm. EF Core, pool size 100, no traffic spike. How do I diagnose this?
evals.expected_output: Incident Analysis with mode label, all 9 steps (Symptoms → Root Cause → BotE → Fix → Prevention → Lesson → Pattern → Decision Rule → Knowledge Capture), BotE pool math showing connections exhausted, concrete EF Core fix, and KOS record generation.

assertions.text: Response includes 'Mode: Incident Analysis' or 'Incident Analysis' as a heading
assertions.name: contains-mode-label

assertions.text: Response contains BotE pool math with numbers (connections x hold_time vs pool_size)
assertions.name: contains-bote-pool-math

assertions.text: Response names a specific root cause (not just 'check your code' or generic advice)
assertions.name: identifies-root-cause

assertions.text: Response gives concrete EF Core-specific fix (e.g. AsNoTracking, using disposal, connection leak fix)
assertions.name: includes-ef-core-fix

assertions.text: Response mentions generating or creating KOS records (incident log capture)
assertions.name: mentions-kos-capture

evals.prompt: Review this design: .NET API receives order webhooks, saves to PostgreSQL, publishes to Kafka. Go consumer reads Kafka, updates order status. No retry logic on the consumer, no DLQ. ~50 orders/minute today, expecting 10x growth.
evals.expected_output: System Design Review with mode label, BotE (QPS, storage, concurrency ceiling), scores for all 7 dimensions (Flow Completeness, Failure Handling, Data Consistency, Retry & Idempotency, Observability, Scalability, Security Boundary), risks ranked by likelihood x impact, and an action plan.

assertions.text: Response includes 'Mode: System Design Review' or 'System Design Review' as a heading
assertions.name: contains-mode-label

assertions.text: Response scores all 7 dimensions: Flow Completeness, Failure Handling, Data Consistency, Retry & Idempotency, Observability, Scalability, Security Boundary
assertions.name: scores-7-dimensions

assertions.text: Response explicitly flags missing retry logic AND missing DLQ as risks
assertions.name: flags-missing-retry-dlq

assertions.text: Response contains BotE numbers for the 10x growth scenario (e.g. 500 orders/min, QPS calculation)
assertions.name: contains-bote-numbers

assertions.text: Response ends with a ranked action plan or prioritized next steps
assertions.name: has-action-plan

evals.prompt: We need to decide between synchronous REST calls vs Kafka events for communication between our .NET order service and Go fulfillment service. Orders come in at ~200/minute today, expected to reach 2,000/minute in 6 months. Fulfillment processing takes 2–5 seconds. What should we choose?
evals.expected_output: Architecture Decision in ADR format with mode label, BotE (QPS ceiling, latency budget), both options with trade-offs and BotE impact, a concrete decision with justification, expected outcome with target numbers, and risks to watch.

assertions.text: Response includes 'Mode: Architecture Decision' or ADR-style heading
assertions.name: contains-mode-label

assertions.text: Response contains BotE latency budget calculation given 2-5s fulfillment time
assertions.name: contains-bote-latency-budget

assertions.text: Response covers both REST and Kafka options with explicit trade-offs for each
assertions.name: covers-both-options-with-tradeoffs

assertions.text: Response picks one option and gives a specific justification (not just pros/cons without a conclusion)
assertions.name: gives-concrete-decision

assertions.text: Expected outcome section includes target numbers (not just qualitative statements)
assertions.name: has-expected-outcome-numbers

evals.prompt: I've been a senior developer for 3 years. I write code daily but haven't led any system design sessions, written any ADRs, or formally documented incident post-mortems. I want to move toward a Software Architect role. Where should I focus first?
evals.expected_output: Career Roadmap with mode label, 6-indicator Progress Check scored, highest-leverage gap identified, connection to competency domain, one concrete action specific enough to start today, and offer to update career-roadmap.md.

assertions.text: Response includes 'Mode: Career Roadmap' or 'Career Roadmap' as a heading
assertions.name: contains-mode-label

assertions.text: Response scores or checks all 6 indicators: ADRs written, KOS incidents logged, system reviews done, runbooks generated, trace ID adopted, design reviews led
assertions.name: runs-progress-check

assertions.text: Response names a single highest-leverage gap (not a generic list of 10 things to do)
assertions.name: identifies-highest-leverage-gap

assertions.text: Response gives a next action specific enough to start today (not 'read books about architecture')
assertions.name: gives-concrete-next-action

assertions.text: Response offers to update career-roadmap.md with evidence from this session
assertions.name: offers-roadmap-update
